title: Multimodal precision neuroimaging of the individual human brain at ultra-high fields
abstract: Multimodal neuroimaging, in particular magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), allows for non-invasive examination of human brain structure and function across multiple scales. Precision neuroimaging builds upon this foundation, enabling the mapping of brain structure, function, and connectivity patterns with high fidelity in single individuals. Highfield MRI, operating at magnetic field strengths of 7 Tesla (T) or higher, increases signal-to-noise ratio and opens up possibilities for gains spatial resolution. Here, we share a multimodal Precision Neuroimaging and Connectomics (PNI) 7T MRI dataset. Ten healthy individuals underwent a comprehensive MRI protocol, including T1 relaxometry, magnetization transfer imaging, T2*-weighted imaging, diffusion MRI, and multi-state functional MRI paradigms, aggregated across three imaging sessions. authors: Cabalo, D. G.; Leppert, I. R.; Thevakumaran, R.; Dekraker, J.; Hwang, Y.; Royer, J.; Kebets, V.; Tavakol, S.; Wang, Y.; Zhou, Y.; Benkarim, M. O.; Eichert, N.; Paquola, C.; Tardif, C. L.; Rudko, D.; Smallwood, J.; Cruces, R. R.; Bernhardt, B.
